perturbation.original_text: Upon expiration, this Second Amendment shall be of no further force or effect, and the terms and conditions of the Agreement shall as they were before the Second Amendment Effective Date. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, a Party's right to enforce the terms and conditions of this Second Amendment shall survive the Second Amendment's expiration.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon expiration, this Second Amendment shall be of no further force or effect, and the terms and conditions of the Agreement shall as they were before the Second Amendment Effective Date. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, a Party's right to enforce the terms and conditions of this Second Amendment shall not survive the Second Amendment's expiration.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says 'a Party's right to enforce the terms and conditions of this Second Amendment shall survive the Second Amendment's expiration.' while the modified text it directly contradicts it stating 'a Party's right to enforce the terms and conditions of this Second Amendment shall not survive the Second Amendment's expiration.' This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the enforceability of the agreement's terms after its expiration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. This Second Amendment shall be effective from The Second Amendment Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2021. Upon expiration, this Second Amendment shall be of no further force or effect, and the terms and conditions of the Agreement shall as they were before the Second Amendment Effective Date. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, a Party's right to enforce the terms and conditions of this Second Amendment shall not survive the Second Amendment's expiration.
perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts the original intent by stating that a party's right to enforce the terms of the Second Amendment 'shall not survive' its expiration. The contradicted text, which is actually the same text block, contains the opposite assertion: 'Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, a Party's right to enforce the terms and conditions of this Second Amendment shall not survive the Second Amendment's expiration.' This creates a clear and irreconcilable conflict within the contract regarding the enforceability of the agreement after its termination.

perturbation.original_text: Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A and Schedule A-1 attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Products," each individual unit of Product "Unit"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's facilities (the "Facilities"). [***], except that, notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, [***].
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A and Schedule A-1 attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Products," each individual unit of Product "Consignment"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's facilities (the "Facilities"). [***], except that, notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, [***].
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines the term 'Unit' as each individual unit of Product. By changing this definition to 'Consignment', it contradicts the later use of 'Unit' in the amended Section 2(a) and subsequent sections where the original meaning is assumed. This creates confusion regarding what 'Unit' refers to, particularly concerning pricing and quantity obligations elsewhere in the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2(d) of the Agreement is amended so that whereas it previously read: "During the Term, Premier shall have the right (but not the obligation) to order from Heritage quantities of Products in excess of [***] and provided Heritage has the capacity and the ability to produce such additional quantities of Products, Heritage agrees to produce such additional quantities per the pricing and terms on Schedule C." it now reads: "During the Term, Premier shall have the right (but not the obligation) to order from Heritage quantities of Products in in excess of [***] and provided Heritage has the capacity and the ability to produce such additional quantities of Products, Heritage agrees to produce such additional quantities per the pricing and terms on Schedule C."
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Unit' as an individual unit of Product. The changed text redefines 'Unit' as 'Consignment.' The contradicted text, Section 2(d), refers to 'quantities of Products.' Since the meaning of 'Unit' has been altered, it creates a contradiction because the number of 'Products' (units) is no longer clearly defined, leading to potential disputes on pricing and quantity obligations outlined in Schedule C, thus creating a strong, direct, and impactful contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered by Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A and/or Schedule A-1, which Schedules may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), shall conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E, and shall conform in all material respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage.
perturbation.changed_text: All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered by Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A and/or Schedule A-1, which Schedules may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), shall not conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E, and shall conform in all material respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires that 'All Products ... shall conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E'. In the modified version, this is changed to 'shall not conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E', creating a direct contradiction. This leaves unclear whether products must or must not meet the expectations outlined in Schedule E.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2(o)(i) of the Agreement is amended so that the first sentence that previously read: "All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered to Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A and/or Schedule A-1, which Schedules may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), shall conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E, and shall conform in all material respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage." now reads: "All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered by Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A and/or Schedule A-1, which Schedules may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), shall not conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E, and shall conform in all material respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage."
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text (which is now the contradicted text) regarding whether the products must conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual. The original text states that they 'shall conform', whereas the changed text states that they 'shall not conform'. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict, making it unclear whether the products are required to meet the expectations outlined in Schedule E, thus making the contract unenforceable with respect to that particular requirement.
